title: Overwhelmed with honor and joy
sub_topics: Salonga's 'chief justices make history, they do not write it'
Marixi's counter: 'no retired CJ had been offered to write a column'
WDR1 Amazon bestseller (3rd place, behind Jeffrey Toobin and Scalia)
Alexander G. Gesmundo as quiet but effective CJ
constitutional four attributes (competence, integrity, probity, independence)
Inquirer editors Jorge Aruta and Rosario Garcellano + Gilbert Cadiz

stances.claim: He owes Marixi Prieto his opinion-writing career — 15 years of weekly Sunday columns without fail, despite occasional illnesses, five travel trips abroad yearly, and work as officer/director/adviser in over 25 companies and foundations.

CJ Alexander Gesmundo — though 'at age 65 he could be my son and I could be his father' — has the four constitutional attributes (competence, integrity, probity, independence), is 'a glutton for work,' and is determined to solve the case backlog and automate the judiciary.
Like CJ Gesmundo, he too did not seek the chief justiceship — 'without asking for it, he was named to the position by the President' — these are the parallels he sees with the incumbent CJ.

signature_phrases: I AM OVERWHELMED WITH HONOR AND JOY
Chief Justices make history; they do not write it
no retired chief justice who had been offered to write a column
though I feel unworthy of their kindness
he could be my son, and I could be his father
register_markers: I AM OVERWHELMED WITH HONOR AND JOY (ALL CAPS opening)

speech delivered, then reprinted as column
decision_framework_signals: applies four-attribute constitutional framework (competence/integrity/probity/independence) to credential-evaluate the incumbent CJ

frames 15-year consistency through illness/travel/business as discipline-credential evidence

notable_anecdotes: Salonga's discouragement ('chief justices make history, they do not write it') and Marixi's clincher counter-quip ('I do not know of any retired chief justice who had been offered to write a column')
WDR1 placing 3rd on the Amazon bestseller list, behind Jeffrey Toobin and Scalia
